note: Government assistance programs in one place. Federal programs apply nationwide; NY / NYC entries are detailed because New York is our first fully-covered state. Always confirm details on the official link — rules and amounts change.

screeners.name: Benefits.gov
screeners.what: Official screener that checks you against 1,000+ federal & state benefit programs in a few minutes.

screeners.name: ACCESS HRA (NYC)
screeners.what: NYC's one-stop screen & apply tool for SNAP, Cash Assistance, and more.

screeners.name: myBenefits (NY State)
screeners.what: New York State's screening & application portal for SNAP, HEAP, and tax credits.

screeners.name: Call 211
screeners.what: Free, confidential statewide help line — a real person points you to local food, rent, utility, and crisis help.

documents: Photo ID (driver license, IDNYC, or passport)
Social Security number(s) for everyone applying
Proof of income — recent pay stubs, award letters, or a statement of no income
Proof of where you live — lease, rent receipt, or a utility bill
Proof of expenses — rent/mortgage, utilities, childcare, medical bills

programs.name: Cash Assistance (Family Assistance / Safety Net)
programs.what: Monthly cash help for families and individuals with little or no income.
programs.apply: Apply on ACCESS HRA, at an HRA Job Center, or via myBenefits.

programs.name: One-Shot Deal (Emergency / One-Time Assistance)
programs.what: A one-time emergency grant for a sudden crisis you can't cover — back rent to stop an eviction, a utility shutoff, moving costs, or replacing essentials after a fire/flood. You don't have to be on Cash Assistance to apply, and it can be a grant or a repayable loan depending on your situation.
need: An emergency you can't pay for
Proof of the emergency (eviction notice, shutoff notice, etc.)
programs.apply: Apply on ACCESS HRA ("Emergency Assistance / One-Time Assistance") or at an HRA Job Center — bring the proof of your emergency.

programs.name: Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
programs.what: A refundable tax credit that puts money back in your pocket if you worked and earned a low-to-moderate income — worth thousands for families.
need: File a tax return (even if you owe nothing)
programs.apply: Claim it when you file taxes; free filing help at IRS VITA sites or nyc.gov/taxprep.

programs.name: Supplemental Security Income (SSI)
programs.what: Monthly cash for people who are 65+, blind, or disabled and have very limited income/resources.
programs.apply: Apply with the Social Security Administration online, by phone, or at a local office.

programs.name: Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher
programs.what: A voucher that pays part of your rent in private housing; you generally pay ~30% of income.
programs.apply: Through your local Public Housing Authority; in NYC via NYCHA or HPD when waitlists open.

programs.name: NYCHA Public Housing
programs.what: City-owned apartments with rent capped at ~30% of household income.
programs.apply: Apply through NYCHA's self-service portal.

programs.name: CityFHEPS rental assistance
programs.what: A rent supplement that helps people who are homeless or facing eviction get and keep housing.
need: Referral often via shelter or Homebase
programs.apply: Through HRA, a shelter, or a Homebase office.

programs.name: Homebase (homelessness prevention)
programs.what: Free help to avoid eviction and stay housed — rent arrears help, mediation, benefits, job support.
programs.apply: Walk into your neighborhood Homebase office or call 311.

programs.name: Housing Choice / HUD programs
programs.what: Find every HUD rental, public-housing, and homeownership program and your local housing authority.
programs.apply: Use HUD's program & local-PHA finder.

programs.name: SNAP (food stamps)
programs.what: Monthly money on an EBT card to buy groceries.
programs.apply: Apply on ACCESS HRA (NYC), myBenefits (NY), or your local SNAP office.

programs.name: WIC
programs.what: Food, formula, nutrition help, and breastfeeding support for pregnant people, new parents, and kids under 5.
programs.apply: Find your local WIC office through the NY Health Dept.

programs.name: Free & reduced school meals
programs.what: Free or low-cost breakfast and lunch at school; many NYC schools serve all students free.
programs.apply: Through your child's school or district.

programs.name: Find a food pantry
programs.what: Free groceries and meals near you, today, no long application.
programs.apply: Search by ZIP, or dial 211.

programs.name: Medicaid
programs.what: Free or low-cost health coverage for people with low income.
programs.apply: Enroll through NY State of Health.

programs.name: Child Health Plus
programs.what: Low-cost or free health insurance for kids under 19 who don't qualify for Medicaid.
programs.apply: Enroll through NY State of Health.

programs.name: NY State of Health marketplace
programs.what: Compare and enroll in health plans; many people qualify for big subsidies.
programs.apply: Apply online, by phone, or with a free in-person Navigator.

programs.name: Medicare
programs.what: Federal health coverage for people 65+ and some younger people with disabilities.
programs.apply: Enroll through Social Security / Medicare.gov.

programs.name: HEAP (Home Energy Assistance)
programs.what: Helps pay heating bills in winter, plus emergency benefits if your heat is shut off or about to be.
programs.apply: Apply on myBenefits (NY) or through your local HEAP/DSS office.

programs.name: Lifeline (phone/internet)
programs.what: A monthly discount on phone or internet service for low-income households.
need: On a qualifying program (SNAP/Medicaid/SSI) or low income
programs.apply: Apply through the Lifeline National Verifier.

programs.name: Utility arrears & shutoff help
programs.what: Programs and protections to clear past-due gas/electric bills and stop shutoffs.
programs.apply: Contact your utility about an arrears/relief program, or call 211; a One-Shot Deal may also cover it.

programs.name: Child Care Assistance / vouchers
programs.what: Help paying for childcare so you can work, look for work, or attend school.
programs.apply: Apply through your local Social Services district (ACS in NYC).

programs.name: Head Start / Early Head Start
programs.what: Free early education, health, and family support for kids 0–5 in low-income families.
programs.apply: Find a local program through the Head Start locator.

programs.name: Unemployment Insurance
programs.what: Temporary weekly payments if you lost your job through no fault of your own.
programs.apply: File with the NY Department of Labor.

programs.name: Social Security Disability (SSDI)
programs.what: Monthly payments if a disability keeps you from working and you've paid into Social Security.
programs.apply: Apply with the Social Security Administration.
